title: How the symbolism could have made more sense
display_title: How the symbolism could have made more sense
wikitext:
[]: How the symbolism could have made more sense             Quote Post by Timstuff » Fri Aug 11, 2006 11:10 pm A lot of the religious symbolism in Evangelion is kind of mish-mashed and jumbled, but the other day I was thinking about how it could have made a little more sense. Suppose that Adam and Lilith had their names switched. By doing this, Lilith would be the "mother" of the angels, and "Adam" would be the projenitor of the human race, and in a roundabout way, the first human. However, it wouldn't stop there. Humans would loose their "lilim" title, and instead, the Angels would be changed to the Lilim, rather than the angels. Accoridng to the most common variety of the Lilith myth, Lilith after leaving Adam went on to fornicate with a demon, and the destiny of her children was to torment and kill the children of Adam and Eve. Think about that-- the "lilim" and humans would never be able to co-exist, and one would inevitably destroy the other-- sounds an awful lot like the angels and humans from Evangelion, no? So basically in my mind, if Adam and Lilith's names were switched, and the Angels were changed to "Lilim" (with humans not having a special second name), then the symbolism seems to make more sense, but obviously that's not what's in the Anime. So am I just thinking too hard?

[]:             Quote Post by AuthenticM » Fri Aug 11, 2006 11:26 pm It's the other way around. It makes more sense the way it is now. Lilith was banished outside of the garden of Eden because she did not want to submit to Adam during sex intercourses (message behind this [written by the fucks who started the religion/myth/nonsense in the first place] is Man > woman ). So, she went and had sex with demons, and bore more demons known as Lilim. My point is, it makes perfect sense for Lilith to be the Mother of mankind, because Humans are demons, in every sense of the word. And what are the humans/Lilim/demons doing in NGE? They're killing Angels (symbolism here...), because that's what they were born to do. That's my take on it, and was/is probably Anno's take on it too since he's made the thing this way.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Fri Aug 11, 2006 11:51 pm When you look at it with that interperetation... Wow, it makes even less sense! In Judeo-Christian lore, there are three parties at play: God, Satan, and humans. Clearly, one of them is absent in NGE's symbology. What's confusing is which one it is, because pretty much what's happened is that the names are switched around and combined without any particular thought to it. To make sense, the "Angels" would have been lilim, and humans would be (duh) humans, with Angels being absent (and since pure-blood demons would be absent as well, that leaves just Humans against Lilim). And the reason why? According to Anno, it's because he didn't expect NGE to be so popular in western culture, and they didn't think any Japanese people would notice inconsistancies in the symbolism, since there aren't many Christians in Japan (and pretty much no Jews at all). It was pretty much an afterthought to differenciate it further from Gundam, and they didn't really put a lot of deep thought into it (which is what Anno himself says). Anno actually at one point said that if he'd known Eva would be so popular in the west, the symbolism might have been a little different, because as it is there's a lot of confusion when we try to compare it to the bible and the Lilith stories. There isn't really a "right" answer to the percieved inconsistancies, because Gainax wasn't expecting them to be a problem.

[]:             Quote Post by felineki » Sat Aug 12, 2006 12:08 am You're kinda forgetting the point that the Angels, Lilim, Adam, Lilith, and the beings who created the latter two are ALL human. There is no "God" in the strictest sense.

[]:             Quote Post by AuthenticM » Sat Aug 12, 2006 12:09 am Timstuff wrote: When you look at it with that interperetation... Wow, it makes even less sense! In Judeo-Christian lore, there are three parties at play: God, Satan, and humans. Clearly, one of them is absent in NGE's symbology. What's confusing is which one it is, because pretty much what's happened is that the names are switched around and combined without any particular thought to it. To make sense, the "Angels" would have been lilim, and humans would be (duh) humans, with Angels being absent (and since pure-blood demons would be absent as well, that leaves just Humans against Lilim). And the reason why? According to Anno, it's because he didn't expect NGE to be so popular in western culture, and they didn't think any Japanese people would notice inconsistancies in the symbolism, since there aren't many Christians in Japan (and pretty much no Jews at all). It was pretty much an afterthought to differenciate it further from Gundam, and they didn't really put a lot of deep thought into it (which is what Anno himself says). Anno actually at one point said that if he'd known Eva would be so popular in the west, the symbolism might have been a little different, because as it is there's a lot of confusion when we try to compare it to the bible and the Lilith stories. There isn't really a "right" answer to the percieved inconsistancies, because Gainax wasn't expecting them to be a problem.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Sat Aug 12, 2006 12:15 am Don't sweat it, no insult was taken. ;) I just wanted to point out that with Evangelion lacking a third party, it's very difficult to compare it directly to the original judeo-christian stories in it's current form, which is likely due to the fact that the symbolism wasn't thought out very thoughouly when NGE was being made.

[]:             Quote Post by AuthenticM » Sat Aug 12, 2006 12:33 am Assistant director Kazuya Tsurumaki wrote: Timstuff wrote: which is likely due to the fact that the symbolism wasn't thought out very thoughouly when NGE was being made.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 12, 2006 12:36 am Timstuff, you're paraphrasing Tsurumaki, not Anno. They're different dudes. Hopefully you're not forgetting the basic situation in NGE: Adam got to Earth first and was supposed to give the planet to her children, a race of 'gods' (informally known as the "Angels"), "the true successors". However, Lilith crashed the party in her Black Moon, Adam somehow got screwed over, and Lilith-spawn got the planet instead. The "final phase" of Lilith-based life were those that achieved human status -- us, the Lilim, the "false successors". So, what do Lilim do? Hunt the children of Adam! Well, I guess we suck too much to actually do that per se, so instead we do things to their Mommy and she goes asplode, "awaken" them, and set a nice, big trap. Then, when they come 'round and take the bait, we slaughter them one after the other without any regard for their status as fellow human beings. How kind of us. But some of them were gonna do bad things to us anyway. The symbolism is all a bit loose. I suppose one thing to remember about Adam and Lilith is that they were created as equals, from the same stuff, but things didn't turn out so sweet, what with Lilith condemned to lose tons of her infant demon-spawn on a daily basis and having to even things out by going after Adam-larvae. (Pray the parents don't have those nifty amulets!) Geez, you two, couldn't you have just put a little variety into your sex life, then everyone could have been happy! NGE's Adam and Lilith were created equals, too, although things, yet again, didn't go the way they should have.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Sat Aug 12, 2006 12:45 am Hmm... Since Shinji and Asuka are technically the son and daughter of Eva (or Evas, in this case), what does that make them? :shock: Now I'm really making things confusing.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 12, 2006 1:02 am You only have to worry about that if babies come out of the actual Evas.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Sat Aug 12, 2006 1:07 am I suppose that may be true. But still, what would the offspree of Evas be called, if we're assuming that Humans = demons and Angels = humans? Would the children of Evas be angels that are reffered to as demons? It seems to be the only option avalible.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sat Aug 12, 2006 6:24 am Timstuff wrote: I suppose that may be true. But still, what would the offspree of Evas be called, if we're assuming that Humans = demons and Angels = humans? Would the children of Evas be angels that are reffered to as demons? It seems to be the only option avalible.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 12, 2006 12:08 pm Ohhhh, I'm sure one could find an appropriate esoteric name for them. But... who would the "daddies" be? That would be an important determining factor! (In a never-finished NGE spin-off of mine, the Evas start a new race with various lucky Apostles [= Adam's kids]; the Evas get 'knocked up' in a few different ways. I just called the resultant offspring 'Yaliyd', perhaps incorrect Hebrew for 'descendants'. The harpies got left out of the main festivities, so I let one of them have an illegitimate lovechild with Kyoko's son. )

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sat Aug 12, 2006 1:19 pm Reichu wrote: Ohhhh, I'm sure one could find an appropriate esoteric name for them. But... who would the "daddies" be? That would be an important determining factor! (In a never-finished NGE spin-off of mine, the Evas start a new race with various lucky Apostles [= Adam's kids]; the Evas get 'knocked up' in a few different ways. I just called the resultant offspring 'Yaliyd', perhaps incorrect Hebrew for 'descendants'. The harpies got left out of the main festivities, so I let one of them have an illegitimate lovechild with Kyoko's son. )

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Aug 13, 2006 5:06 am Reichu wrote: Ohhhh, I'm sure one could find an appropriate esoteric name for them. But... who would the "daddies" be? That would be an important determining factor! (In a never-finished NGE spin-off of mine, the Evas start a new race with various lucky Apostles [= Adam's kids]; the Evas get 'knocked up' in a few different ways. I just called the resultant offspring 'Yaliyd', perhaps incorrect Hebrew for 'descendants'. The harpies got left out of the main festivities, so I let one of them have an illegitimate lovechild with Kyoko's son. )

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 13, 2006 10:45 am Legend wrote: Heh. No end to the insanity, is there?

[]:             Quote Post by felineki » Sun Aug 13, 2006 1:09 pm Reichu wrote: That particular bit of insanity has been over for a while.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 13, 2006 1:27 pm It's officially been in limbo since the end of last year. Might even be dead.
